nameMeanings.name: Johan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Older Scottish spelling of Joan , which was traditionally pronounced as two syllables.

variants.name: Jan
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: Jon
meanings: Simplified spelling of   John   or a short form of   Jonathan .  The name is borne by the Canadian singer  Jon Vickers  (  b. 1926 ) and the American actor  Jon Voight  (  b. 1938 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Emrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Eric
meanings: Of Old Norse origin, from ei ‘ever, always’ (or einn ‘one, alone’) + ríkr ‘ruler’ ( see Eirik ). It was introduced into Britain by Scandinavian settlers before the Norman Conquest. As a modern given name, it was revived in the mid 19th century and has remained in use since.

en.lifeSummary: Johan Emrich Neidhofer was born in 1648, in Singhofen, Unterlahnkreis, Hesse-Nassau, Prussia, Germany. He married Anna Dorothea in 1665, in Singhofen, Singhofen, Unterlahnkreis, Hesse-Nassau, Prussia, Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

ru.lifeSummary: Johan Emrich Neidhofer родился в 1648 г. в местности сингхофен, унтирлахнкрис, хессен-нассау, Пруссия, Германия. Он женился на женщине по имени Anna Dorothea в 1665 г. в местности сингхофен, сингхофен, унтирлахнкрис, хессен-нассау, Пруссия, Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
